instruction:
Using the orthogonalization method, find the first three orthogonal polynomials $P_{0} \left( \begin{array} {c} {{x}} \\ \end{array} \right), P_{1} \left( \begin{array} {c} {{x}} \\ \end{array} \right), P_{2} \left( \begin{array} {c} {{x}} \\ \end{array} \right)$ on the interval $\left[ 0, 1 \right]$ with the weight $\rho( x )=\operatorname{l n} \frac{1} {x}$
as ______.

Options:
A. $$
P_{0} \left( x \right)=1
$$
$$
P_{1} ( x )=x-\frac{1} {3}
$$
$$
P_{2} ( x )=x^{2}-\frac{2} {5} \, x+\frac{1 6} {1 4 4}
$$
B. $$
P_{0} \left( x \right)=x
$$
$$
P_{1} ( x )=x-\frac{1} {4}
$$
$$
P_{2} ( x )=x^{2}-\frac{5} {9} \, x+\frac{1 9} {2 2 8}
$$
C. $$
P_{0} \left( x \right)=1
$$
$$
P_{1} ( x )=x-\frac{1} {5}
$$
$$
P_{2} ( x )=x^{2}-\frac{4} {9} \, x+\frac{2 1} {2 5 6}
$$
D. $$
P_{0} \left( x \right)=1
$$
$$
P_{1} ( x )=x-\frac{1} {2}
$$
$$
P_{2} ( x )=x^{2}-\frac{3} {8} \, x+\frac{1 3} {1 8 2}
$$
E. $$
P_{0} \left( x \right)=x
$$
$$
P_{1} ( x )=x-\frac{1} {5}
$$
$$
P_{2} ( x )=x^{2}-\frac{2} {7} \, x+\frac{1 8} {2 2 5}
$$
F. $$
P_{0} \left( x \right)=x
$$
$$
P_{1} ( x )=x^{2}-\frac{1} {3}
$$
$$
P_{2} ( x )=x^{3}-\frac{4} {9} \, x+\frac{1 5} {3 6}
$$
G. $$
P_{0} \left( x \right)=1
$$
$$
P_{1} ( x )=x-\frac{1} {3}
$$
$$
P_{2} ( x )=x^{2}-\frac{2} {6} \, x+\frac{2 3} {2 9 5}
$$
H. $$
P_{0} \left( x \right)=1
$$
$$
P_{1} ( x )=x-\frac{1} {4}
$$
$$
P_{2} ( x )=x^{2}-\frac{5} {7} \, x+\frac{1 7} {2 5 2}
$$
I. $$
P_{0} \left( x \right)=x
$$
$$
P_{1} ( x )=x-\frac{1} {4}
$$
$$
P_{2} ( x )=x^{2}-\frac{5} {7} \, x+\frac{1 6} {2 4 2}
$$
J. $$
P_{0} \left( x \right)=1
$$
$$
P_{1} ( x )=x-\frac{1} {6}
$$
$$
P_{2} ( x )=x^{2}-\frac{4} {7} \, x+\frac{1 5} {3 1 2}
$$
